calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jan-04-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Gene Benitez, Esq. 415-550-7213 for Plaintiff.
Nils Rosenquest, Esq. 415-292-0980 of Rosenquest & Associates for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Mr. Rosenquest and Mr Benitez argue. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Defendant's claim of exemption is denied. A business bank account is not subject to the "tools of the trade" exemption. (See C.F. Nielsen, Inc. v. Stern (1992) Cal.App.4th Supp. 22 [attorney's business bank account not an exempt CCP 704.060 tool of the trade].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Dec-19-2022 Continued To Jan-04-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nils Rosenquest, Esq., ROSENQUEST & ASSOCIATES, 415-292-9080, rosenquest@earthlink.net, for Defendant Addy Wong,
No other appearances

Defendant's counsel did not properly contest the court's tentative ruling. Defendants notice of contest received after 4 p.m. Friday 12-16-22,

Not ARGUED. The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is continued to January 4, 2023. Defendant's counsel to provide notice to plaintiffs' counsel of the new hearing date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2021 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing Of Default Prove-Up
Off Calendar Feb-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Addy Wong.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Andrew Safont, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Ibarra Professional Law Corporation.
Crisostomo Ibarra, a witness for and principal at Ibarra Professional Law Corporation, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Safont & Ibarra appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Addy Wong.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract and Common Counts was filed on August 16, 2019, and was personally served on defendant Addy Wong on August 31, 2019.
Default as to defendant Addy Wong was entered on December 5, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed on August 3, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Ibarra Professional Law Corporation, a corporation, and against the defaulted defendant Addy Wong, a corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $18,509.20.
Prejudgment Interest - $2,215.59.
Attorney Fees - $2,520.00.
Costs - $462.00.

Total - $23,706.99.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
